sarga.title: Rama, further enquires Viswamitra narrates the birth of Tataka, her marriage, curse, etc., and convinces Rama to slay her.

shlokas.original:
अथ तस्याप्रमेयस्य मुनेर्वचनमुत्तमम्।
श्रुत्वा पुरुषशार्दूल: प्रत्युवाच शुभां गिरम्।।1.25.1।।
shlokas.translation: On hearing the excellent words of Viswamitra, a sage with unimaginable faculty. Rama, the tiger among men (Rama) replied in a, gentle voice:

shlokas.original:
अल्पवीर्या यदा यक्षा श्श्रूयन्ते मुनिपुङ्गव।
कथन्नागसहस्रस्य धारयत्यबला बलम्।।1.25.2।।
shlokas.translation: "O best of ascetics, I have heard the yakshas possess little prowess. How can a woman who is by gender weak possess the strength of a thousand elephants?

shlokas.original:
विश्वामित्रोऽब्रवीद्वाक्यं शृण येन बलोत्तरा। 
वरदानकृतं वीर्यं धारयत्यबला बलम्।।1.25.3।।
shlokas.translation: Viswamitra said, "Listen, how she acquired the prowess and strength by virtue of a boon she received.

shlokas.original:
पूर्वमासीन्महायक्षस्सुकेतुर्नाम वीर्यवान्।
अनपत्यश्शुभाचारस्स च तेपे महत्तप:।।1.25.4।।
shlokas.translation: "In the past there was a powerful and great yaksha by name Suketu. He had no  children. So he performed an intense penance following virtuous practices.

shlokas.original:
पितामहस्तु सुप्रीतस्तस्य यक्षपते स्तदा।
कन्यारत्नं ददौ राम ताटकां नाम नामत:।।1.25.5।।
shlokas.translation: Rama the grandsire Brahma was highly gratified and granted to the king of yakshas, the gem of a daughter by name known asTataka.

shlokas.original:
ददौ नागसहस्रस्य बलं चास्या: पितामह:।
नत्वेव पुत्रं यक्षाय ददौ ब्रह्मा महायशा:।।1.25.6।।
shlokas.translation: The illustrious grandsire Brahma granted her the strength of a thousand elephants,  but did not bestow a son on the yaksha.

shlokas.original:
तां तु जातां विवर्धन्तीं रूपयौवनशालिनीम्।
झर्झपुत्राय सुन्दाय ददौ भार्यां यशस्विनीम्।।1.25.7।।
shlokas.translation: As she grew up, glowing with youth and beauty she was given in marriage to Sunda, son of Jharjha.

shlokas.original:
कस्यचित्त्वथ कालस्य यक्षी पुत्रमजायत।
मारीचं नाम दुर्धर्षं यश्शापाद्राक्षसोऽभवत्।।1.25.8।।
shlokas.translation: After some time that yakshini gave birth to an unassailable son by name Maricha. Because of a curse, he became a rakshasa (though born of yaksha parentage).

shlokas.original:
सुन्दे तु निहते राम सागस्त्यमृषिसत्तमम्।
ताटका सह पुत्रेण प्रधर्��यितुमिच्छति।।1.25.9।।
shlokas.translation: O Rama when Sunda died Tataka along with her son wanted to attack Agastya, the best of sages.

shlokas.original:
भक्षार्थं जातसंरम्भा गर्जन्ती साभ्यधावत।0
आपतन्तीं तु तां दृष्ट्वा अगस्त्यो भगवानृषि:।।1.25.10।।
राक्षसत्वं भजस्वेति मारीचं व्याजहार स:। 1
shlokas.translation: Roaring, she rushed with excitement to devour him. The venerable  rishi Agastya saw her approaching him and cursed Maricha to assume form of a demoness.

shlokas.original:
अगस्त्य: परमक्रुद्धस्ताटकामपि शप्तवान्।।1.25.11।।
पुरुषादी महायक्षी विरूपा विकृतानना।
इदं रूपं विहायाथ दारुणं रूपमस्तु ते।।1.25.12।।
shlokas.translation: Mighty angry, Agastya cursed Tataka saying, 'Abandon this form of a great yakshini and assume the terrible figure of a rakshasi, a cannibal with distorted appearance and a hideous countenance'.

shlokas.original:
सैषा शापकृतामर्षा ताटका क्रोधमूर्छिता।
देशमुत्सादयत्येनमगस्त्यचरितं शुभम्।।1.25.13।।
shlokas.translation: Enraged at the curse, senseless, Tataka with anger, has been ravaging this sacred land when Agastya walked.

shlokas.original:
एनां राघव दुर्वृत्तां यक्षीं परमदारुणाम्।
गोब्राह्मणहितार्थाय जहि दुष्टपराक्रमाम्।।1.25.14।।
shlokas.translation: O Rama for the welfare of cows and brahmins, slay this yakshini who is wicked, extremely cruel and possessing vile prowess.

shlokas.original:
न ह्येनां शापसम्स्पृष्टां कश्चिदुत्सहते पुमान्।
निहन्तुं त्रिषु लोकेषु त्वामृते रघुनन्दन।।1.25.15।।
shlokas.translation: O son of Raghu's dynasty she is maligned by the curse, and so not a single man in the three worlds except you, is  competent to slay this yakshini.

shlokas.original:
न हि ते स्त्रीवधकृते घृणा कार्या नरोत्तम।
चातुर्वण्यहितार्थाय कर्तव्यं राजसूनुना।।1.25.16।।
shlokas.translation: O best among men you need not hate killing a woman. You are a prince. You should serve the interest of the four orders of the society.

shlokas.original:
नृशंसमनृशंसं वा प्रजारक्षणकारणात्।
पातकं वा सदोषं वा कर्तव्यं रक्षता सता।।1.25.17।।
shlokas.translation: Whether cruel or kind, sinful or wrong whatever contributes to the protection of the subjects, should be done by the righteous (king).

shlokas.original:
राज्यभारनियुक्तानामेष धर्मस्सनातन:।
अधर्म्यां जहि काकुत्स्थ धर्मोह्यस्या न विद्यते।।1.25.18।।
shlokas.translation: This is the eternal law binding those who are appointed to bear the burden of a kingdom. O Kakutstha kill her. She knows no dharma.

shlokas.original:
श्रूयते हि पुरा शक्रो विरोचनसुतां नृप।
पृथिवीं हन्तुमिच्छन्तीं मन्थरामभ्यसूदयत्।।1.25.19।।
shlokas.translation: O Prince It is heard that in the past Indra slew Manthara, Virochana's daughter who intended to destroy the earth.

shlokas.original:
विष्णुनापि पुरा राम भृगुपत्नी दृढव्रता।
अनिन्द्रं लोकमिच्छन्ती काव्यमाता निषूदिता।।1.25.20।।
shlokas.translation: 'O Rama! In olden times, Kavya, the mother of Sukracharya, the wife of sage Bhrigu, was destroyed by Vishnu determined to destroy Indra.'

shlokas.original:
एतैश्चान्यैश्च बहुभी राजपुत्र महात्मभि:।
अधर्मसहिता नार्यो हता: पुरुषसत्तमै:।।1.25.21।।
shlokas.translation: O Prince by these magnanimous persons and by great men several unrighteous women were killed for the common good". इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये बालकाण्डे पञ्चविंशस्सर्ग:।। Thus ends the twentyfifth sarga of Balakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
